message: Stage -1.2 drafted angle=0 v1 mode=cold-start runner=codex/gpt-5.5 status=completed
Sharp computable binary-IV ATE envelope with simultaneous defier and never-taker caps.

Review boundary stage_0.5_to_0 routed to stage_neg1 (reject fast-path): Case 6b (unsalvageable conjecture at flagship floor) + regression pattern (a) — both reviewer rounds reject on the same novelty-tier defect: the proposal as conjectured reduces to a useful subfield-tier finite-LP specialization with two mass caps, and the stronger five-family facet catalogue / strict-shrinkage frontier that would close the flagship gap was first only partially confirmed and then overclaimed via generic LP-duality language. The reviewer is explicit that flagship requires either a constructive exposed-basis enumeration (which Codex tried and failed to produce honestly) OR positioning against a named published open regime (which the conjecture's framing against Balke–Pearl / Richardson–Robins / symbolic discrete-bound methods does not deliver). Re-deriving the same conjecture at Stage 0 would burn budget on the same novelty-floor defect, not a fixable derivation step.
Stage -0.5 NO-PASS — pivot budget exhausted (3 angles tried)
